title: Automation
summary: Record or draw parameter changes over time in Arrangement view. Used for filter sweeps into drops, volume fades, FX movements, and anything that needs to change during the track.
steps: Press A to toggle Automation view in Arrangement view — automation lanes appear below each track
Click the automation lane selector (dropdown on the left of each lane) to choose which parameter to automate (Volume, Pan, a device parameter, etc.)
Draw automation: select the Pencil tool (B key) and click to add breakpoints. Drag breakpoints to set the value. Right-click a breakpoint to delete it
Draw straight lines between breakpoints: click one breakpoint, hold Shift, click another — creates a linear ramp
Record automation live: press the Automation Arm button (red A in the transport), press Play, then move a parameter with your mouse or MIDI controller
Cmd+click any parameter in the device view to jump directly to its automation lane in Arrangement view
tips: Filter sweep into a drop: add AutoFilter to a pad, open its Cutoff automation lane, draw a ramp from 200Hz to full open over the last 8 bars before the drop
Automation is per-clip in Session view (Clip Envelopes) and per-timeline in Arrangement view. Arrangement automation is more powerful for full-track movements
To remove automation from a parameter: right-click its automation lane > Clear Automation. Or select all breakpoints and delete
Automation snaps to the grid by default. Hold Cmd to draw freehand automation that ignores the grid — useful for volume fades and smooth curves
practice_hint: In any session in Arrangement view, add an AutoFilter to a pad track. Press A to view automation, select the Cutoff parameter, and draw a ramp from 300Hz to 20kHz over 8 bars. Play through that section to hear the sweep.
ableton_version_note: Available in all editions of Live 9+.
